business.headline: Holi 2026: Is stock market open or closed today?The stock market is closed on March 3, on account of Holi 2026. While the exchanges have declared the holiday for Tuesday, the festival of colours will be observed across many parts of the country on Wednesday, March 4.

European gas futures jump 42% after Qatar halts LNG amid Middle East conflictEuropean natural gas prices have surged sharply due to the halt in LNG supplies caused by Middle East conflict. This disruption highlights Europe's growing energy vulnerability amid geopolitical tensions.

QatarEnergy to halt LPG production as Middle East tensions deepenThe crisis entered its third day as Iran widened its retaliation following joint US–Israeli strikes that killed its Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, along with several senior military and political figures.

If 50% of insurers offer mental health cover, why is usage below 20%?While it is encouraging that mental health cover is becoming more common, the low usage rate raises questions. Are employees fully aware of what is available to them? Do they know how to access these services? Or are they still hesitant to seek help?
EPFO's interest stays at 8.25% for FY26The last change in the rate came in 2024, when it was raised from 8.15% to 8.25% for 2023-24. Since then, there has been no further increase or reduction.
Sensex ends 1,048 lower, Nifty below 24,900; RIL down 2%The S&P BSE Sensex lost 1,048.34 points to close at 80,238.85, while the NSE Nifty50 tanked 312.95 points to end at 24,865.70.

From LPG prices to UPI rules: 3 key March 2026 changes that could impact your budgetMarch 2026 has brought a few important changes that could affect your monthly budget. From LPG cylinder prices to new UPI rules and railway updates, here are three key changes you should know about.

Strait of Hormuz is buzzing again and why it matters to the global economyThe Strait of Hormuz is a narrow waterway between Iran and Oman that connects the Gulf to the Arabian Sea. On a typical day, ships carrying oil equal to about one-fifth of global demand pass through it.

Rs 9 lakh crore gone as brutal Dalal Street crash rattles investorsThe sell-off on Dalal Street has erased over Rs 9 lakh crore in investor wealth, dragging BSE's total market capitalisation down from nearly Rs 463 lakh crore to around Rs 454 lakh crore during intraday trade.

Bitcoin at $66,000 mark: 3 things crypto investors must know as Iran–US tensions riseBitcoin is hovering around the $66,000 level as global markets reel from rising Iran–US tensions. However, the key question remains: what's next?

Sensex nosedives 1,500 points today: Should investors be worried?Markets are sliding under Middle East tensions, surging crude, US–Iran hostilities and fears of trade blockages. Fundamentals look steady for now, but if the conflict deepens, is this only the first shock?

Gold jumps Rs 6,000, silver soars Rs 10,000: How Iran–US tensions affect your moneyGold and silver surged as Iran–US tensions drove investors towards safer assets. The big question is, what does this mean for your money?

RVNL, IRFC down up to 5%: Why are these railway stocks falling today?RVNL was down 5.74% to Rs 298.20 at around 12:03 pm, while IRFC shares were down 4.65% to Rs 98.74. The weakness in these names comes at a time when sentiment across domestic equities has turned cautious as global risk appetite faded and benchmark indices weakened.

L&T share price tumbles over 5%. Why is the stock falling today?L&T's slide highlights how surging crude and geopolitical uncertainty are unsettling infrastructure stocks, prompting investors to reassess cost risks and project visibility until conditions stabilise in conflict-hit regions.

Paras Defence shares jump 12%: Why are defence stocks rising today?Defence stocks across the board were firm even as the rest of the market struggled with weak sentiment linked to rising crude prices and the worsening conflict involving Iran and the United States.

Sensex tumbles 1,048 points amid Iran-US conflict: 6 things you need to knowThe S&P BSE Sensex fell sharply in early trade, and the NSE Nifty50 also slipped more than 2%, as investors reacted to the fast-changing situation between the US, Israel and Iran.

Sensex tanks 1,000 points as oil surges amid ongoing Iran-US conflictThe S&P BSE Sensex fell over 1,000 points in early trade, slipping below 81,000. The NSE Nifty50 dropped under 24,900. This was one of the steepest opening declines in recent weeks.

Will stock market fall today amid Iran's war with the US, Israel?Early signals from Gift Nifty suggest a weak start. Gift Nifty futures were trading at 25,60 points at 8:30 am. This indicates that the benchmark Nifty 50 may open below Friday's close of 25,178.65.

Sufficient stock, India won't face immediate oil disruption amid Iran crisisIndia imports 88 per cent of its crude oil – the raw material for making fuels like petrol and diesel – and about half of its requirement for natural gas - the feedstock for generating electricity, producing fertiliser, fuel for household kitchens and CNG for automobiles – and cooking gas LPG.

entertainment.headline: Laughs, tears and surprises: Inside Rashmika Mandanna-Vijay Deverakonda's sangeet Vijay Deverakonda and Rashmika Mandanna shared heartfelt moments during their sangeet ceremony. Their joyous celebrations featured emotional speeches, romantic dances and stunning outfits, setting the tone for their wedding.

Dalimb first look: Jitendra Kumar takes on darker role for psychological thriller The first look of Jitendra Kumar's psychological thriller Dalimb was unveiled by ZEE5 on Holi, marking his shift into a darker and more intense role.

Rashmika-Vijay announce scholarships to students in his hometown after wedding Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda visited his native village in Telangana, Thummanpet, to perform post-wedding pujas and visit his new home. They pledged scholarships for Class 9 and 10 students across 44 government schools to support their education.

The Kerala Story 2 box office Day 4: Film sees dip, earns Rs 12.8 crore The Kerala Story 2: Goes Beyond, which released amid controversy, witnessed a significant drop compared to the first installment. The Hindi film's total earnings now stand at Rs 12.8 crore after four days.

Love & War: Alia Bhatt calls working with Bhansali magical, shares filming update Alia Bhatt revealed that Love & War, directed by Sanjay Leela Bhansali, is nearing completion and described the experience as "magical."

Did you know Bridgerton's Luke Thompson has Indian roots? Actor reacts Bridgerton actor Luke Thompson revealed his Indian roots and expressed a strong desire to explore the country, ahead of the release of Bridgerton Season 4.

Not educated, but fiercely free: Tamil cinema finds feminist icons in matriarchs Two recent Tamil films, Thaai Kizhavi and Angammal, spotlight older women defying patriarchal norms with fierce independence. These stories reveal feminism rooted in survival and everyday rebellion against family pressures.

His visit was planned: Cesar Awards dismiss Jim Carrey's impersonation rumours Jim Carrey's appearance at Cesar Awards set social media abuzz with some users bizarrely claiming he had been impersonated. As speculation swirled online, the delegate of the awards issued an official statement addressing the controversy and putting the rumours to rest.

Eric Dane's cause of death confirmed as respiratory failure linked to ALS Actor Eric Dane's cause of death is finally out. The Grey's Anatomy actor died from respiratory failure, with Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) identified as an underlying cause.

R Madhavan shares details on free housing for passengers amid Dubai airspace closure R Madhavan praised Dubai government's initiative to bear all expenses of the stranded passengers amid airspace closure due to Middle East tensions.

Watch: Allu Arjun, Nagarjuna, Atlee at Allu Sirish-Nayanika's pre-wedding bash Allu Sirish and Nayanika Reddy's pre-wedding party was attended by Allu Arjun, Nagarjuna, Atlee, Nandamuri Balakrishna and others. The couple will get married on March 6, 2026.

Vivek Oberoi, in Dubai, urges people not to spread panic amid Middle East tensions Vivek Oberoi, who is currently in Dubai, called for peace and unity amid tensions in the Middle East. He urged people not to spread panic in these sensitive times.

Ranbir Kapoor had a witty jibe for paps getting too close at event. Read it here Actor Ranbir Kapoor was in the mood for banter when a photographer tried to get too close for a shot at the Indian Super Gaming League.

Why Arijit Singh left Mumbai to return to hometown Jiaganj: Singer's father reveals Arijit Singh's father, Surinder Singh recalled their family's journey from Lahore to Jiaganj, West Bengal, after Partition. He opened up on why Arijit is always drawn back to his hometown.

Toxic song Tabaahi: Yash, Kiara's romantic track is out minus video, fans react The first audio single from Yash and Kiara Advani's upcoming film Toxic: A Fairytale for Grown-Ups has been released. The track, titled Tabaahi, is available in several languages, but the music video has not been unveiled. This has prompted disappointment among fans.

Nora Fatehi says she's safe in India, and Middle East visuals have shaken her Actor Nora Fatehi, who resides in Dubai, has addressed her followers amid the ongoing Middle East conflict, stating that she is currently safe in India. She called for global unity while expressing concern over recent violent developments.

Mandana Karimi calls Iranian regime cancerous, talks about Middle East crisis Actor Mandana Karimi has spoken about the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, sharing her personal distress and condemning the Iranian regime, which she called a 'cancerous cell' in the region.

Chris Hemsworth credits blunt honesty for lasting marriage with Elsa Pataky Chris Hemsworth opened up about the one rule that keeps his marriage to Elsa Pataky strong, and why her honesty matters more than praise.

K-drama Exclusive: Park Min-young, Wi Ha-jun ready for Bollywood on one condition Siren's Kiss showcases the nuanced acting talents of Park Min-young and Wi Ha-jun, who bring their characters' keen observational skills to life with compelling chemistry. The duo also share their admiration for Indian cinema, with Min-young expressing excitement about a potential Bollywood debut inspired by the magic of Aladdin, in an exclusive chat with India Today.

general news.headline: Dubai-bound flights return to India as escalating Iran-US war derails resumptionAs regional airspace remains volatile, airlines are recalibrating operations on a day-to-day basis. With lakhs of Indians living and working in the Gulf, broader evacuation or normalisation of services will depend on how the security situation unfolds.

Silence on Khamenei's killing not neutral but abdication: Sonia Gandhi slams govtThe former Congress president also demanded that when Parliament reconvenes for the second part of the Budget session, the government's "disturbing silence" over the breakdown of international order must be debated openly and without evasion.

Iran's war is with US, Israel. But why are Gulf states being attacked?Over the course of 4 days, Iran has launched a barrage of missiles targeting at least nine Gulf countries and US military bases located there. What's behind the move? Will it pay dividends for Iran?

From traffic cameras to phone networks, how Israel tracked Khamenei: Financial TimesNearly all traffic cameras in Tehran had allegedly been compromised for years, the Financial Times reported, adding that even the mobile phone systems were infiltrated to track Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

Why Pakistan set its own house on fire amid Iran infernoHandcrafted ByOur Editors The war thrust upon Afghanistan by Asim Munir and Shehbaz Sharif, the unchecked protests, and the killing of protesters at the US consulate in Karachi have the one same motive. Deny Trump the pound of flesh he might seek during the Iran war.

Live: Gulf countries intercept hundreds of Iranian missiles as conflict escalatesUS-Israel Iran Strikes Live Updates: Today is the fourth day of the direct military confrontation between US-Israel forces and Iran. It continues to intensify in the Middle East, with the US launching airstrikes across Iran targeting the country's government and military leadership, while Tehran retaliates by striking American bases and interests in the Gulf, escalating the crisis into a broader regional conflict. US President Donald Trump on Monday refused to rule out the deployment of American ground forces, asserting that Washington is prepared for a prolonged campaign, even as Iran maintained that it would not bow to pressure and insisted it is ready to negotiate with the US. Iran also announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, warning that any vessel attempting to pass through the strategic waterway would be set ablaze. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates on the escalating military conflict in the Middle East.

Is BJP's Suvendu Adhikari planning to take on Mamata in her Kolkata bastion?The 2021 West Bengal Assembly Elections saw a high-stakes battle in the Nandigram constituency, as Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee unsuccessfully took on turncoat and BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari. In the 2026 election, Adhikari could bring the fight to Mamata by challenging her in Bhabanipur, the TMC chief's Kolkata bastion.

OpenAI fixes sloppy US Military deal after ChatGPT uninstalls surge by almost 300%OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has admitted that the AI startup looked opportunistic and sloppy for rushing to get a deal with the Pentagon after the US government decided to terminate Anthropic's contracts.

The ace who loves a joke: The two Sides of Arshdeep SinghT20 World Cup 2026: Arshdeep Singh brings laughter to the dressing room and calm to India's toughest moments. Behind the easy smile is a bowler who has quietly earned the team's deepest trust.

Not going to have endless war: Netanyahu as Middle East on boil for 4th dayIsraeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said the US-Israeli strikes on Iran would be "quick and decisive," asserting the campaign is not an endless war but a step toward lasting peace in the Middle East.

Indian-origin investor meets Trump at Mar-a-Lago before Iran strikes, shares detailsRecounting her experience at Mar-a-Lago, Indian-American venture capitalist Asha Jadeja Motwani said she was part of a small group of around 20 of US President Donald Trump's friends who were seated around a dinner table at the Florida resort as tensions with Iran escalated.

How America's bomb factories laid foundation of the fertiliser revolutionThe global fertiliser revolution began in American military plants that manufactured bombs during World Wars I and II. After the wars ended, ammonia and ammonium nitrate plants were converted for fertiliser production. In the podcast "Annagatha" on Kisan Tak, Dr. Yashpal Sehrawat of the International Fertiliser Development Centre (IFDC) explained how explosive-making technology later became the foundation of modern agriculture.

Laughs, tears and surprises: Inside Rashmika Mandanna-Vijay Deverakonda's sangeetVijay Deverakonda and Rashmika Mandanna shared heartfelt moments during their sangeet ceremony. Their joyous celebrations featured emotional speeches, romantic dances and stunning outfits, setting the tone for their wedding.

Iran shuts key oil route Strait of Hormuz, vows to burn ships that try to passThe announcement marks one of the sharpest geopolitical escalations in the ongoing armed conflict between Iran on one side and Israel and the US on the other.

2 Iranian drones hit US Embassy in Riyadh, Trump warns 'you'll find out soon'Two Iranian drones struck the US Embassy in Riyadh, triggering a fire and some material damage, as the escalating US-Iran-Israel conflict entered its fourth day. US President Donald Trump warned that retaliation was imminent.

Not educated, but fiercely free: Tamil cinema finds feminist icons in matriarchsTwo recent Tamil films, Thaai Kizhavi and Angammal, spotlight older women defying patriarchal norms with fierce independence. These stories reveal feminism rooted in survival and everyday rebellion against family pressures.

Kidulting this Holi: Grown-ups spend 'adult money' on super-cool pichkarisNot just kids, but grown-ups are splurging, spending what they jokingly call their 'adult money' on the coolest designs they can find.

Trump's flip on 'boots on ground in Iran' as US Embassy in Riyadh attackedUS President Donald Trump said he was not ruling out deploying American ground troops to Iran if required. His remarks came as Iranian drones struck the US Embassy in Riyadh, prompting him to warn Tehran of imminent retaliation.

Holi 2026: Is stock market open or closed today?The stock market is closed on March 3, on account of Holi 2026. While the exchanges have declared the holiday for Tuesday, the festival of colours will be observed across many parts of the country on Wednesday, March 4.

Pakistan President Zardari claims India preparing for another war, urges talksThe Pakistani president also criticised India's decision to place the Indus Waters Treaty in abeyance, calling it plain and simple hydro-terrorism and accusing New Delhi of weaponising water flows for political leverage.

Rare lunar eclipse to illuminate skies today, paint the Moon blood-redA rare total lunar eclipse will turn the Moon blood red tonight. Know the science behind a Blood Moon, and find out the exact timings and the best ways to watch this celestial event from India.

West Indies, Zimbabwe stranded in Kolkata due to Middle East conflictThe West Indies cricket team are stranded in Kolkata after their T20 World Cup exit. The development comes due to airspace closures caused by tensions between Israel and Iran in the Middle East.

Eric Dane's cause of death confirmed as respiratory failure linked to ALSActor Eric Dane's cause of death is finally out. The Grey's Anatomy actor died from respiratory failure, with Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) identified as an underlying cause.

Depart now! US asks Americans to leave over a dozen Middle East countriesMora Namdar, US State Department's assistant secretary for consular affairs, said US citizens should leave using available commercial transportation 'due to safety risks.' The warning came after the department, in recent days, updated its travel advisories for several countries in the region to recommend against travel.

sports.headline: 'Big game pacer' Jasprit Bumrah backed to deliver for India in semi-final ICC T20 World Cup: Australian fast-bowling great Glenn McGrath has termed Jasprit Bumrah a big-game player, backing the star pacer to deliver for India in the semi-final against England at Mumbai's Wankhede Stadium on March 5.

Virat Kohli will forever be RCB's icon and hero: Mo Bobat Royal Challengers Bengaluru director of cricket Mo Bobat has praised Virat Kohli, saying the former India captain will always remain RCB's icon and hero after spending nearly two decades with the franchise in the Indian Premier League.

The ace who loves a joke: The two Sides of Arshdeep Singh T20 World Cup 2026: Arshdeep Singh brings laughter to the dressing room and calm to India's toughest moments. Behind the easy smile is a bowler who has quietly earned the team's deepest trust.

Women's football team sent undersized jerseys, forced to buy local kits Days before their AFC Women's Asia Cup opener, the Indian team was hit by a jersey fiasco as the All India Football Federation (AIFF) made a big blunder by sending the squad the wrong kits.

Well experienced in India conditions, New Zealand ready for SA challenge at Eden T20 World Cup: New Zealand are set to take on South Africa in the semi-final 1 of the T20 World Cup 2026. New Zealand's Daryl Mitchell is confident that their experience in Indian conditions will keep them well prepared going into the clash against South Africa.

Wounded Barcelona ready to make 'impossible, possible' against Atletico Madrid Barcelona face a daunting 0-4 deficit in the Copa del Rey semi-final second leg against Atletico Madrid. Hansi Flick has called upon his team to make the impossible, possible.

West Indies, Zimbabwe stranded in Kolkata due to Middle East conflict The West Indies cricket team are stranded in Kolkata after their T20 World Cup exit. The development comes due to airspace closures caused by tensions between Israel and Iran in the Middle East.

Captain? Won't even keep him in the team: Afridi lashes out at Pakistan player Former Pakistan captain Shahid Afridi remains furious at the team. He has lashed out at one of the players and said that, let alone captaincy, he does not even deserve a place in the team.

Is Sehwag unprofessional? Fans call out ex-batter over viral Miller remarks Former India batter Virender Sehwag's comment about David Miller has gone viral on the internet. Many cricket fans are calling Sehwag unprofessional due to the nature of his admission.

The IND vs PAK handshake row: What do the outsiders think ft. Bavuma, Vaughan Temba Bavuma says the India–Pakistan no-handshake gesture does not look good for cricket, while admitting he is not fully aware of the political context behind the decision.

Mohammad Amir is not wrong. India are yet to show up as a teamSanju Samson's unbeaten 97 rescued India from T20 World Cup elimination against West Indies, sparing careers. Yet, batting woes, Bumrah-dependent bowling, and butterfingers fielding expose deep cracks heading into the England semi-final.

Enjoyed the cupcake ad: South Africa coach takes dig at World Cup promoSouth Africa head coach Shukri Conrad has revealed that the team was well aware of the cupcake advertisement made by T20 World Cup broadcaster Star Sports. Conrad took a sly dig at the ad ahead of their semi-final clash against New Zealand.

FIFA chief calls for red cards to players covering mouths after Prestianni racism rowFIFA president Gianni Infantino says players who cover their mouths while confronting opponents could face red cards if linked to racist abuse after the Vinicius Junior incident.

FIFA World Cup 2026: Iran's participation uncertain amid ongoing military conflictIran's football federation president remains doubtful whether the national team can fulfil its World Cup fixtures in the United States after U.S.-Israeli strikes heightened regional tensions.

The night Sanju Samson fulfilled his prophecyIND vs WI, T20 World Cup: Sanju Samson played the best innings of his career to guide India to the semi-final of the T20 World Cup 2026. Samson, who debuted for India in 2015, finally fulfilled his prophecy.

Pakistan board fines players Rs 16 lakh each after T20 World Cup failure: ReportPakistan Cricket Board has reportedly imposed a Rs 16 lakh fine on each player following their early exit from the 2026 T20 World Cup. Pakistan failed to go past the Super 8 stage in the tournament.

Justice by Samson: Irfan Pathan says wicketkeeper reminded everyone of KohliT20 World Cup: Irfan Pathan said that Sanju Samson reminded everyone of Virat Kohli with his knock in the India vs West Indies clash on Sunday, March 1. Samson's knock helped India reach the semi-finals of the T20 World Cup.

Rohit Sharma rates 2007 final as his favourite India-Pakistan clashIndia's great Rohit Sharma relived some of his fondest memories from past T20 World Cup clashes against Pakistan, reflecting on a rivalry that has defined the tournament over the years.

Sanju Samson should be a regular in India's white-ball setup: Sourav GangulyT20 World Cup: Sourav Ganguly heaped praise on Sanju Samson for his match-winning knock against the West Indies on Sunday, March 1. Samson was the star of the show as India won the match by five wickets.

After talks with Zaheer Khan, BCCI approaches Harbhajan Singh for off-spinners campBCCI has approached former India greats Zaheer Khan and Harbhajan Singh for specialised camps at the Centre of Excellence in Bengaluru. VVS Laxman initiated talks to help groom the next generation.

You will get your chance: Rohit's message to Samson before T20 WC goes viralT20 World Cup: Rohit Sharma's message to Sanju Samson ahead of the T20 World Cup 2026 goes viral after the wicketkeeper helped India defeat West Indies in Kolkata. Samson's knock helped India reach the semi-final of the tournament.

Stranded in Dubai, Sindhu set to miss All England. Lakshya to lead India's campaignTwo-time Olympic medallist PV Sindhu is unlikely to participate in the prestigious All England Open Super 1000 tournament, which begins on Tuesday. The Indian shuttler has been stranded at Dubai airport amid escalating conflict in the Middle East following US-Israel strikes on Iran.

Prague Chess: D Gukesh implodes, loses to Abdusattorov to remain winlessGukesh's nightmare in Prague continued as time trouble cost him a winning position against Abdusattorov. The World Champion blundered twice in the endgame to suffer his fifth straight setback.

The smell of burnt rubber, the deafening roar: Watching an F1 car live hits differentYou can follow F1 for years. But nothing prepares you for the moment an F1 car fires up metres away, and the sound sends shivers down your spine.

Spain vs Argentina Finalissima in Qatar at risk after US, Israel attacks on IranEscalating tensions in the Middle East have cast uncertainty over the highly anticipated Finalissima between Spain and Argentina, originally set for March 27 in Doha. The Qatar Football Association's suspension of all tournaments, along with delays to other regional matches, has led organisers to reconsider the feasibility of hosting the event amid recent air strikes from US and Israel on Iran.

India to host Afghanistan for one Test and a three-match ODI series in June 2026The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) on Monday confirmed that Afghanistan are set to visit India in June 2026 to play a standalone Test and a three-match One-Day International (ODI) series.

Sanju Samson's Kolkata blitz on par with Kohli's MCG knock vs PAK: SrikkanthT20 World Cup: Kris Srikkanth has claimed that Sanju Samson's innings in Kolkata is on par with the knock played by Virat Kohli at the MCG in the T20 World Cup 2022 against Pakistan. Samson's 97 helped India reach the semi-finals of the T20 World Cup 2026.

Switched off phone, stayed away from social media: Sanju on handling tough phasesIndia opener Sanju Samson switched off his phone and stayed away from social media to safeguard his self-belief during a lean patch, a phase that ended with a match-winning knock in the do-or-die T20 World Cup clash against the West Indies.

The batter who came in from the cold: Sanju Samson's night, Eden Gardens' legendT20 World Cup, India vs West Indies: Sanju Samson had long been the backup, the nearly man—the gifted cricketer forever waiting for his turn. On Sunday night at Eden Gardens, Samson made sure no one would ever doubt him again.

Rulayega kya pagle? Sanju Samson's hilarious reaction to Surya's praises: VideoT20 World Cup: Suryakumar Yadav's praises were so high for Sanju Samson that the Indian wicketkeeper had to step in and ask the Indian captain not to make him cry in hilarious fashion. Samson's sensational knock helped India make it to the semi-final of the T20 World Cup.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
